2603.23269.title: Not All Tokens Are Created Equal: Query-Efficient Jailbreak Fuzzing for LLMs
2603.23269.paper_summary: Large Language Models(LLMs) are widely deployed, yet are vulnerable to jailbreak prompts that elicit policy-violating outputs. Although prior studies have uncovered these risks, they typically treat all tokens as equally important during prompt mutation, overlooking the varying contributions of individual tokens to triggering model refusals. Consequently, these attacks introduce substantial redundant searching under query-constrained scenarios, reducing attack efficiency and hindering comprehensive vulnerability assessment. In this work, we conduct a token-level analysis of refusal behavior and observe that token contributions are highly skewed rather than uniform. Moreover, we find strong cross-model consistency in refusal tendencies, enabling the use of a surrogate model to estimate token-level contributions to the target model's refusals. Motivated by these findings, we propose TriageFuzz, a token-aware jailbreak fuzzing framework that adapts the fuzz testing approach with a series of customized designs. TriageFuzz leverages a surrogate model to estimate the contribution of individual tokens to refusal behaviors, enabling the identification of sensitive regions within the prompt. Furthermore, it incorporates a refusal-guided evolutionary strategy that adaptively weights candidate prompts with a lightweight scorer to steer the evolution toward bypassing safety constraints. Extensive experiments on six open-source LLMs and three commercial APIs demonstrate that TriageFuzz achieves comparable attack success rates (ASR) with significantly reduced query costs. Notably, it attains a 90% ASR with over 70% fewer queries compared to baselines. Even under an extremely restrictive budget of 25 queries, TriageFuzz outperforms existing methods, improving ASR by 20-40%.
2603.23269.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）被廣泛部署，但對於引發政策違規輸出的越獄提示卻十分脆弱。儘管先前的研究已揭示了這些風險，但它們通常在提示變異過程中將所有標記視為同等重要，忽略了個別標記在觸發模型拒絕中的不同貢獻。因此，這些攻擊在查詢受限的情況下引入了大量冗餘搜索，降低了攻擊效率並妨礙了全面的脆弱性評估。在本研究中，我們對拒絕行為進行了標記級別的分析，觀察到標記的貢獻高度不均勻，而非均勻。此外，我們發現拒絕傾向在不同模型之間具有強烈的一致性，這使得可以使用替代模型來估計標記對目標模型拒絕的貢獻。受到這些發現的啟發，我們提出了TriageFuzz，一個具備標記感知的越獄模糊測試框架，通過一系列定制設計來調整模糊測試方法。TriageFuzz利用替代模型來估計個別標記對拒絕行為的貢獻，從而能夠識別提示中的敏感區域。此外，它還結合了一種拒絕引導的進化策略，通過輕量級評分器自適應地加權候選提示，以引導進化過程繞過安全約束。在六個開源LLM和三個商業API上的大量實驗表明，TriageFuzz在顯著降低查詢成本的同時，達到了可比的攻擊成功率（ASR）。值得注意的是，與基線相比，它在查詢數量減少超過70%的情況下達到了90%的ASR。即使在極其嚴格的25次查詢預算下，TriageFuzz也超越了現有方法，將ASR提高了20-40%。
2603.23269.author: Wenyu Chen et.al.
2603.23269.authors: Wenyu Chen, Xiangtao Meng, Chuanchao Zang, Li Wang, Xinyu Gao, Jianing Wang, Peng Zhan, Zheng Li, Shanqing Guo
